intent: tmux feels really basic. I want to feel like my tmux is a cockpit — session restoration, easier pane resizing, maybe a status bar with git info.

suggestions.title: Surface tmux's built-in pane-resize hotkeys you already have
suggestions.rationale: Your tmux.conf.j2 already defines `prefix + Ctrl-h/j/k/l` as repeatable resize bindings (`bind -r`). Hold Ctrl after the prefix and tap h/j/k/l to nudge panes 5 cells at a time. No plugin needed — worth demoing before adding anything new.

suggestions.title: Add tpm (Tmux Plugin Manager) so the other addons become trivial
suggestions.rationale: Session restoration, smart pane resize, and a git-aware status bar all ship as tpm plugins. tpm is the standard loader and unlocks the rest of this list with a single `prefix + I` to install. No existing tpm presence in your tmux.conf.j2.

suggestions.title: tmux-resurrect + tmux-continuum for automatic session restoration
suggestions.rationale: resurrect snapshots windows/panes/working directories on demand (`prefix + Ctrl-s` / `Ctrl-r`); continuum adds periodic auto-save and auto-restore on tmux start. This is the canonical 'cockpit that survives a reboot' setup.

suggestions.title: jaclu/tmux-menus for a discoverable popup menu of resize/layout actions
suggestions.rationale: Instead of memorizing resize bindings, `prefix + \` opens a popup with pane resize, split, kill, layout, and window-move actions. Very 'cockpit HUD' for anyone who prefers discovery over muscle memory.

suggestions.title: Embed git branch + dirty state in your existing tmux statusline
suggestions.rationale: Your status-left already has hostname and session segments. We can add a git segment driven by a small shell script (branch + dirty-dot) called from a `#(...)` block — no extra plugin, keeps your TokyoNight powerline theme intact. We'll use the chr(0xE0A0) Python approach from your tmux README to avoid LLM-corrupting the Nerd Font glyphs.

suggestions.title: tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control for saner, symmetric pane management
suggestions.rationale: Adds `prefix + H/J/K/L` (shifted) for larger 5-cell resizes and `prefix + |` / `-` for intuitive vertical/horizontal splits that keep cwd. Complements your existing vim-style nav without conflicting.

suggestions.title: Add `sesh` as a fuzzy session switcher/creator backed by fzf
suggestions.rationale: You already have fzf and tmux installed but no fast session launcher. `sesh` (joshmedeski) lists tmux sessions, zoxide dirs, and git repos in an fzf popup — pick one and it attaches or creates it. Makes prefix+s feel like a spacecraft comms panel.

suggestions.title: Add `tmuxinator` for declarative project layouts
suggestions.rationale: Define a YAML per project (windows, panes, start commands) and spin up the whole cockpit with `mux start myproject`. Pairs well with resurrect but targets a different need — boot a known-good layout rather than resume whatever was there.
